title: Vintage Curry takes over as Dubs extend season
content: The Warriors, led by Stephen Curry, erased a 13-point deficit in the fourth quarter to edge the Clippers and advance in the NBA's play-in tournament.

title: Minus Embiid, Maxey propels Sixers into playoffs
content: With Joel Embiid watching from the bench, Tyrese Maxey scored 31 points to lead the Sixers past the Magic 109-97 and lock up a first-round playoff date with the rival Boston Celtics.

title: Hornets' Ball fined $35K for 'reckless' Bam play
content: The NBA has fined LaMelo Ball $35,000 "for making unnecessary and reckless contact" with Bam Adebayo during Tuesday's play-in game. The Hornets star also was fined $25,000 for using profane language during a TV interview after that game.

title: LIV CEO says season to continue 'at full throttle'
content: Amid reports that Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund might be on the verge of pulling its funding for the league, LIV Golf CEO Scott O'Neil told staff in an email Wednesday that its season will go on "as planned, uninterrupted and at full throttle."

title: Sources: MLBPA fires two top execs with cause
content: The Major League Baseball Players Association fired two top executives with cause Wednesday following an internal investigation, sources told ESPN.

title: Ulberg says he lost UFC title belt celebrating win
content: Newly crowned UFC light heavyweight champion Carlos Ulberg told Fox Sports Australia that he misplaced his title belt while celebrating his victory.

title: MLB celebrates Robinson's legacy, dons No. 42
content: Major League Baseball celebrated the 79th anniversary of Jackie Robinson breaking the sport's color barrier, with every player, coach and umpire wearing his No. 42 on Jackie Robinson Day.

title: Ohtani skips bat, cranks up pitches in 10-K gem
content: Not in the lineup for his start day Wednesday, Shohei Ohtani channeled all his energy into pitching and delivered a six-inning, 10-strikeout start Wednesday night in an 8-2 win over the Mets.

title: 🏈 Kiper's two-round NFL mock draft
content: Mel Kiper Jr. predicts 64 picks across the first two rounds of the draft, including two potential trades, a few QB landing spots and plenty of impact additions.

title: What MLB players really think of automated ball-strike challenge rule
content: ABS has been the talk of the MLB season. Here's what those in the game say about the new rule -- and how it changes their jobs.

title: LeBron outside the top 20?! Steph at 50?! How we ranked this postseason's stars
content: ESPN analyst Zach Kram ranked and tiered the 50 NBA players most likely to make the biggest impacts in the postseason.

title: UCL talking points: Slot's bizarre choices, Bayern now favorites?
content: Arne Slot's decision to start Alexander Isak backfired, badly. ESPN's Julien Laurens, Sam Marsden, Gab Marcotti and Sam Tighe weigh in on that and all the other moments that mattered from the UCL quarterfinal second legs.

title: NHL playoff watch: What's on the line in Wednesday's games
content: There isn't much season left to determine seeding and draft lottery order. Here's what you need to know.

title: Akheem Mesidor's long, long road to the NFL
content: The Miami defensive end once struggled to make an impact in youth football. Now, he's a potential first-round pick.

title: Paul Heyman and the making of a wrestling god
content: The style that Heyman helped pioneer -- violent, confrontational, anti-corporate -- forever changed the course of pro wrestling.

content: With eight title bouts in two nights, WrestleMania 42 is stacked. Get caught up before this weekend's PLE.

content: One team set the curve with an A+. Three got A grades, including Dallas for picking Azzi Fudd. But one got a D.

title: Mascherano exit highlights Miami's delicate, Messi-centric balancing act
content: Javier Mascherano quitting as Inter Miami manager illustrates how hard it's becoming to keep an aging Lionel Messi happy within a team in transition.

title: From New Jersey to Copa del Rey? Matarazzo reaching new heights in Europe
content: Pellegrino Matarazzo could soon be first U.S.-born coach to win a trophy in one of Europe's top five leagues.

title: Overreactions: Can Arsenal, Spurs stop downward spiral?
content: Let's overreact to last weekend's Premier League action, from Arsenal's slip in the title race to alarm bells ringing at Tottenham.

content: Thirty-two Lehigh players batted during a 20-run first inning that set an NCAA record during a 38-6 blowout win over Coppin State.

title: Can Jordan Chiles end her career with an NCAA team title?
content: Jordan Chiles enters her final NCAA championships as the most recognizable face in gymnastics -- and hopes to lead UCLA to its first title since 2018.

title: 'No excuses': How LSU's strength coach became the ultimate example of fortitude
content: In July, a Jet Ski accident caused the amputation of Katie Guillory's foot. But it only strengthened her drive.

content: Tracking news and updates for players entering -- and exiting -- the transfer portal for the 2026-27 season.

title: WNBA free agency and trade tracker: Aces' Wilson signs 3-year, $5M deal
content: WNBA free agency is here. Kelsey Plum is returning to Los Angeles. Kayla McBride is returning to Minnesota.

title: Remembering 'Coach Mac': Jeff Legwold shares his best Dave McGinnis memories
content: Legwold and the late McGinnis formed a bond over combine breakfasts, large trucks, makeshift wallets and bobbleheads.

title: Men's pound-for-pound rankings: Fury misses out, new name joins top 10
content: Tyson Fury's victory in his boxing return wasn't enough to re-enter the top 10. Here are the latest ESPN pound-for-pound rankings.

title: MMA pound-for-pound rankings: Suarez seizes control of a spot in women's top 10
content: Onetime UFC strawweight title challenger secures her spot in the ESPN P4P rankings with dominant wrestling.

title: Crochet? Crow-Armstrong? Which slow starting superstars should cause concern?
content: Do April showers bring May...fantasy points? Some slow fantasy starts might lead to improved production.

title: Draft insight: What to expect from Betts, Fudd and more
content: Which 2026 WNBA draft picks are ready to contribute this season, and which should you stash for the future?

title: Transfer rumors, news: Newcastle to bring Mourinho back to Premier League?
content: Newcastle United could sack manager Eddie Howe and turn to Jose Mourinho. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Newborns left in dustbins, buses, forests: MP tops infant abandonment for 5th straight year
content: Madhya Pradesh leads the nation in infant abandonment cases. Shocking incidents reveal newborns left in dangerous situations, some surviving miraculously. The National Crime Records Bureau highlights this ongoing crisis. Fear, social stigma, and policy pressures drive these extreme acts. Addressing this requires urgent awareness and support systems.

title: 'Dosa, idly' row: From Jitesh's clip to DJ — what sparked CSK vs RCB controversy
content: In the complaint, CSK said the playing of the track "Dosa, idli, sambar, chutney, chutney" during the match was "not in good taste." The song is often used in memes linked to South Indian stereotypes.

title: How change in H-1B selection system has been a boon to employees already in the US
content: US businesses are witnessing significantly higher H-1B visa selection rates, with some reporting over 50% success. This surge follows the Department of Homeland Security's shift from a random lottery to a wage-weighted system, prioritizing higher salary offers. The new rule, effective February 27, 2026, aims to reshape the selection process for skilled foreign workers.

title: 'Religion-based reservation unconstitutional': Amit Shah & Akhilesh Yadav clash over delimitation
content: Home Minister Amit Shah and SP chief Akhilesh Yadav clashed in Parliament over the Women's Reservation Bill. Yadav questioned the urgency without an updated Census, demanding a caste-based enumeration. Shah assured that a caste census would be conducted after the ongoing Census process, while also stating religion-based reservations are unconstitutional.

title: Amid TCS Nashik row, Tech Mahindra denies religious bias allegations at Mumbai’s Goregaon office
content: Tech Mahindra denies allegations of religious bias at its Goregaon office after social media claims surfaced. This follows heightened scrutiny on IT firms, with TCS Nashik facing a police probe over sexual harassment and religious conversion allegations. Nine FIRs have been registered at TCS Nashik, leading to several arrests.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ box office day 28: Film misses Rs 1,100 cr mark by a whisker
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has achieved a historic Rs 1,099.72 crore net in India, becoming the first Hindi film to cross the Rs 1,000 crore mark in a single language. Despite minor fluctuations, the espionage thriller has maintained a strong box office presence for 28 days, with a worldwide gross of Rs 1,733.20 crore.

title: Parents in Portugal, 5-year-old tied to iron gate by cop grandmother; police dept acts
content: An assistant sub-inspector in Faridkot was suspended after allegedly tying her five-year-old granddaughter to a gate. Neighbors intervened after hearing the child wailing and found her hands and feet bound. The ASI, who was caring for the child while her parents were abroad, was suspended after a video of the incident went viral.

title: 'Serial bride & fake dad' flee with grooms' cash, gold near Mumbai
content: Kashimira police apprehended a man and woman for a wedding scam, where the bride would abscond with valuables days after the ceremony.  The duo, targeting divorced men over 40, were involved in five Maharashtra cases.  The man posed as family to arrange marriages, with the woman then fleeing with cash and jewellery.  They have been remanded in custody.

title: Hormuz disruptions hit! LPG imports halve from Feb levels, domestic output also impacted
content: Ongoing disruptions to energy flows from the Gulf linked to the US-Iran war continue to unsettle global oil and gas markets, and a ceasefire in place for about a week has done little to improve the availability of cooking gas.

title: Noida workers protest: UP govt hikes minimum wages, but can exporters afford it?
content: Garment exporters in Uttar Pradesh face a dilemma as a recent wage hike for workers clashes with rising input costs and squeezed margins.  Ongoing labor unrest in Noida factories is causing concern among global buyers, who are seeking clarity on potential shipment delays and may shift orders to competing nations.

title: HAL’s Light Utility Helicopter: Specs, altitude edge and why it matters for India’s Siachen ops
content: In the super high altitude border areas in both the North and the North East, the Cheetah helicopter is not just a machine, its a lifeline for the troops deployed on desolate posts in defence of India. In these areas the helicopter is a lifeline and often the sole mode of transport. As the Chetak and the Cheetah helicopter head towards retirement, a new helicopter India's own Light Utility Helicopter is poised to take their place.

title: 'Saari nazar utaar denge': PSL CEO wards off 'evil eye' for Babar Azam - Watch
content: The clip surfaced after Zalmi’s match in the ongoing 11th edition of the Pakistan Super League and showed a light exchange during a public interaction. "Aaj saari nazar utaar denge," a person is heard saying in the video.

title: Dismissed UP cop kills beggar to fake his own death, burns body, leaves ID near remains
content: A former Uttar Pradesh Police constable has been arrested in Hathras for allegedly murdering a beggar. The accused then burned the beggar's body and placed his own belongings nearby to fake his death. Police uncovered the elaborate plot during their investigation. The dismissed constable, Ramveer Singh, has a history of serious criminal charges across multiple districts.

title: LeT co-founder Amir Hamza shot at in Lahore in second attack within a year
content: Lashkar-e-Taiba co-founder Amir Hamza was shot by unknown gunmen in Lahore, marking a second assassination attempt in less than a year. The 67-year-old, a designated global terrorist, was attacked outside a TV station while accompanied by a Pakistani judge. This incident follows a previous attempt where he survived a shooting last May.

title: Women’s Reservation Bill: How numbers stack up in Lok Sabha and what delimitation could change
content: Parliament is set to debate the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam, aiming for 33% women's reservation in legislatures. This constitutional amendment, passed in 2023, will be implemented after delimitation based on the 2027 Census. While the government champions women's empowerment, opposition raises concerns over the delimitation process and its potential impact on state representation, creating a north-south divide.

title: How this teenager survived a plane crash - injured, alone and in harsh land
content: At 17, Juliane Koepcke survived a plane crash, falling 3,000 meters into the Amazon rainforest. She then spent 11 days navigating the jungle, a place she knew from her childhood, before being found by fishermen. Her survival, marked by resilience and familiarity with her surroundings, remains a powerful testament to endurance.

title: New record! Apple’s India vendors export $2.5 bn components to China; may reach $3.5 bn
content: Several Apple suppliers in India contributed to shipments to China, including Foxconn, Tata Electronics, Tata-owned Pegatron Technology India, Motherson, Salcomp, TRIL Bangalore and Yuzhan Technology.

title: 'Trying to get a little breathing room': Trump says Israel-Lebanon talks to be on Thursday
content: President Trump announced that Israel and Lebanon are set to hold peace talks, aiming to ease long-standing tensions. This follows a historic trilateral meeting hosted by the US, where both nations expressed willingness for direct negotiations, a ceasefire, and a broader peace framework.

title: Touching moment: Kohli stunned as LSG youngster tries to touch his feet - Watch
content: During an interaction after the match, Tiwari bent down to touch Kohli's feet, a gesture used to show respect. Kohli appeared taken aback and responded with a smile. The video has gone viral on social media.

title: CBSE Class 10 result 2026: How did Delhi perform in this year’s board examinations?
content: CBSE has declared the Class 10 results 2026 with a 93.70% pass rate, showing a slight rise over last year and stable national performance. Delhi outperformed the average with 97.39%, led by strong results in East and West regions. Low compartment rates and high participation underline consistent outcomes despite ongoing exam reforms.

title: Another injury blow for CSK, Indian international ruled out of IPL 2026
content: Chennai Super Kings pacer Khaleel Ahmed has been ruled out of IPL 2026 due to a quadriceps injury, dealing a blow to the franchise’s bowling attack.

title: CSK knock BCCI's door, lodge complaint against RCB for 'Dosa idli' song
content: Chennai Super Kings have lodged a complaint with the BCCI against Royal Challengers Bengaluru over alleged misconduct during their IPL 2026 clash. The complaint cites inappropriate in-stadium entertainment, including a song that CSK felt stereotyped their identity, and remarks made by the stadium DJ after player dismissals. This adds to the growing rivalry between the two franchises.

title: Shatrughan Sinha tells Gulshan Grover, ‘you should’ve played Akshaye Khanna's role in Dhurandhar’
content: Gulshan Grover, who is currently promoting his upcoming series Matka King, believes that cinema is witnessing a full-circle moment—with trends, characters and storytelling styles from the past making a strong comeback. The actor also shared a recent anecdote from a dinner with industry veterans, including Shatrughan Sinha, Rakesh Roshan and Shashi Ranjan. He further revealed how Sinha even imagined him in Akshaye Khanna's Rehman Dakait's role in Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar.

title: TCS Nashik office staff told to work from home amid religious conversion, harassment claims
content: TCS has suspended operations at its Nashik BPO center following an escalated probe into alleged sexual harassment and forced religious conversion. Police are investigating an organized pattern of behavior among accused staff, including HR officials, who allegedly targeted financially vulnerable employees. Separately, Infosys issued a statement addressing similar allegations at its Pune facility.

title: Price prediction: Will gold head to Rs 1.65 lakh/10g & silver rise to Rs 2.80 lakh/kg?
content: Gold and silver price prediction today: Gold and silver prices may extend gains in the coming days, says Abhilash Koikkara, Head - Forex & Commodities, Nuvama Professional Clients Group.

title: In patient mix-up, BHU doctors operate on 71-year-old’s hip instead of spine, woman dies
content: A 71-year-old woman, Radhika Devi, underwent a partial surgery for spinal tumour due to patient misidentification at BHU trauma centre. She was mistakenly operated on for hip replacement. After further complications and multiple surgeries, she passed away. An investigation is underway into the gross medical negligence.

title: Comedy of errors in PSL! Chaos unfolds as run-out chance goes begging - Watch
content: The incident took place in the final over of Quetta’s innings. Ali Raza bowled a waist-high no-ball to Usman Tariq on the fourth delivery. On the next ball, which was a free hit, Abrar Ahmed was on strike.

title: India’s longest funicular railway: A once-in-a-lifetime travel experience
content: India's longest funicular railway, spanning 1.2 kilometers, opened in January 2026 near Kalyan, Maharashtra. This new attraction drastically reduces the travel time to the revered Shri Malanggad temple from a two-hour trek to just 10 minutes, making it accessible for all.

title: 60-second study trick that really works for kids, improves memory
content: Education experts unveil a simple '60-Second Teach-Back Trick' to boost memory retention in children. This method, rooted in active recall, encourages students to explain learned topics aloud without notes, strengthening neural pathways and instantly revealing knowledge gaps. Parents can easily implement this at home, fostering deeper understanding and confidence in their kids.

title: Girl from Bhopal slum tops Madhya Pradesh Board Class 12 results
content: Chandni Vishwakarma, an 18-year-old from Bhopal's Bhim Nagar slum, defied destitution to top the Madhya Pradesh Board class 12 commerce exams with 98.8%. Studying under flickering bulbs and facing daily struggles, she achieved her remarkable feat, inspiring underprivileged youth and aiming for financial independence through CA exams.

title: US won’t renew sanctions waiver on Russian, Iranian oil: What it means for India
content: The US will not extend waivers allowing the sale of Russian and Iranian oil already at sea, impacting countries like India that secured supplies during the temporary relief. This move signals a return to tighter enforcement of sanctions, forcing nations to restrategize their energy procurement amidst global supply disruptions.

title: TVK chief Vijay booked ahead of Tamil Nadu polls
content: TVK president Vijay faces a case for allegedly flouting poll permissions. Officials state a roadshow was conducted instead of the approved event, breaching the Model Code of Conduct. Police have registered a case and are investigating, reviewing video evidence to determine the violation's scope. Further action awaits investigation outcomes.

title: JD Vance Urges Pope Leo XIV To Be "Careful About Matters Of Theology"
content: The pope said last week those who believe in Christ  is never on the side of those who "once wielded the sword and today drop bombs."

title: Pakistan, Bangladesh Migrants Pose As Gay To Access UK Asylum: Report
content: Individuals whose work, study or tourist visas are nearing expiry in the UK are being guided to claim they face danger because of their sexual orientation.

title: In A First, Iran Set To Execute Woman Over January Protests
content: Rights groups accuse Iran of using the death penalty as a tool of repression to instil fear among the prople amid war against Israel and the US.

content: A retirement corpus is not a random financial target. It is a replacement plan for your income when your salary stops.

content: The royal meal laid bare how alone Trump stands in his war with Iran, even though he repeatedly declares victory while declaring the conflict is "very close to over".

content: Bansal clarified that the company has revised the grooming policy multiple times over the years, and the version currently circulating online is outdated.

content: Rhythm Panchal operated out of a vacant plot in Malad and held a darbar (court) every Thursday and Saturday from 8 pm to 7 am.

title: Can Iran Legally Collect Tolls On Vessels Passing Strait Of Hormuz?
content: UN Convention on the Law of the Sea provides vessels a right of unimpeded "transit passage" through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: How A US Blockade On Iran Is Forcing Sanctioned Ships To Turn Back
content: Iran-linked vessels appear to have jammed or faked their locations in some instances, complicating an uncertain and risky shipping situation.

title: "38 Fake Cases Against Me": Congress Flags Madhya Pradesh Minister's Remark
content: The remarks have triggered a political row, with the Congress alleging that the minister concealed the information about pending criminal cases in his election affidavit.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Amid Trump's War On The World, How Effective Really Is India's Strategic Autonomy?
content: India certainly cannot mechanistically base its positions on preferred international norms, unmindful of its own direct interests.

content: Parliament saw a face-off between Union Home Minister Amit Shah and Samajwadi Party (SP) chief Akhilesh Yadav over the Women's Reservation Bill.

content: Based on pay commission's proposal, the Centre decides wages for its employees and pensioners for the next decade.

title: New-to-science gecko Cyrtodactylus jayadityai discovered in Tripura, named after researchers’ mentor
content: Cyrtodactylus jayadityai, recorded from lowland forest patches of North Tripura district, showed a significant divergence from its closest relative, Cyrtodactylus tripuraensis

content: What are the pros and cons of efforts to increase fertility rates? Which are the States with a growing population of the elderly? Has it reached a crisis point in some States? content: PM Modi urged people to come together to build a state where "the poor have shelter, food security is ensured, women feel safe, and youth can find jobs without leaving the state."

content: Congress leader K C Venugopal questioned why the proposed changes in the women's quota law were not incorporated when it was passed earlier by Parliament.

title: BJP president Nitin Nabin, 15 others take oath as Rajya Sabha MPs
content: The newly elected MPs represent Assam, Bihar, Haryana, Chhattisgarh, Himachal Pradesh, Telangana, Odisha and Maharashtra.

title: Wage rage escalates in Noida, domestic workers join stir
content: Day after factory workers’ protests turn violent in Noida, 350 arrested; police conduct flag marches; additional forces deployed across industrial area; workers continue to demand improved working conditions; police use lathi charge, tear gas to disperse domestic workers outside high-rise society

title: How Tamil Nadu got 234 Assembly seats: The Delimitation Commission’s calculated departure
content: Under Article 170 of the Constitution and Section 8(b) of the Delimitation Commission Act, 1962, the number of seats in a State Assembly must be an integral multiple of the number of Lok Sabha seats allotted to that State

title: When welfare met demographic concerns
content: A new study revisits the parliamentary debates in the 1960s over linking maternity benefits to population control in India

title: Assam Assembly elections 2026: Bid to woo new voters in CM Himanta’s long-time constituency Jalukbari
content: With Himanta Biswa Sarma criss-crossing the State as campaigner-in-chief, the challenge for local BJP canvassers in Jalukbari is to ensure healthy numbers in order to match or better the CM’s six-figure margin from 2021

title: Andhra Pradesh Fire Services to deploy robot, drones for firefighting in hazardous zones
content: The drones, with a payload capacity of 20 kg, can carry water, extinguishing powder or firefighting equipment, and will be used in emergencies

title: Supreme Court refuses to interfere with Bombay HC verdict lifting stay on fraud proceedings against Anil Ambani
content: A Bench headed by CJI Surya Kant did not intervene against the Division Bench decision to set aside a Single Judge order of December last year

title: Araku MP Thanuja Rani seeks reopening of Chinabhogili railway gate in Parvatipuram-Manyam
content: Araku Member of Parliament Gumma Thanuja Rani urges the railway authorities to reopen the Chinabhogili railway gate, the only route of travel for nearly 10,000 people across five villages of Parvatipuram-Manyam district

title: Watch: Decoding Stalin vs. Shah fight over delimitation
content: Chief Minister M.K Stalin has convened an all-party meeting on March 5 to discuss the impact of the proposed delimitation exercise.

title: Delimitation row: T.N. all-party meet favours retaining existing Lok Sabha seats till 2056
content: CM Stalin says, “It is unjustifiable to reduce the number of Lok Sabha seats of southern States because they have effectively implemented population control measures”

title: Supreme Court refuses to entertain plea seeking guidelines for display of Ashoka Chakra
content: The petitioner showed a picture of the Ashoka Chakra installed at a roundabout in Varanasi, but the Bench refused to entertain the plea

title: Ramesh Chennithala says decision on Kerala CM up to high command, urges end to talks after Sudhakaran’s praise for K.C. Venugopal
content: Senior Congress leader says speculations over who will become the Chief Minister if UDF comes to power will ‘impact the morale’ of party workers

title: Military conflict cannot resolve problems: PM Modi on West Asia, Ukraine after talks with Austria Chancellor
content: The Prime Minister said India and Austria are in agreement that military conflict cannot resolve problems

title: Accident near Mantralayam leaves residents of Hassan village in grief
content: The deceased were part of a group of relatives, from two villages in Hassan district and one village in Chikkamagaluru district, who came together for a trip to Mantralayam in Andhra Pradesh

title: Delimitation and Women’s reservation: Key parties’ stance
content: The plan to link women’s reservation in Parliament with delimitation has created a sharp political divide.

title: Andhra Pradesh Intermediate supplementary exams 2026 time table released; exams from May 21
content: The Board of Intermediate Education, Andhra Pradesh, releases the time table for the Intermediate Public Advanced Supplementary Examinations scheduled May 2026

title: Women’s collective warns parties to ensure 33% women candidates for Assembly polls or face NOTA
content: Thulya Prathinidhya Prasthanam raises five demands ahead of Assembly polls, calls for gender-balanced politics in Kerala.

title: Government sends feelers to Opposition on women’s quota law implementation
content: The government is looking into bringing an amendment to the 106th Constitutional Amendment Act to remove the requirement of delimitation for reserving seats for women in Lok Sabha and State Assemblies

title: Fast‑tracking Women’s Reservation Bill may influence Uttar Pradesh polls
content: The timing of these “feelers” is significant. In 2027, seven States go to polls, four of which are ruled by the BJP

title: Congress urges government to hold an all‑party meeting on women’s quota law
content: The government had reached out to at least two parties — the Congress and the Samajwadi Party — seeking their opinion on advancing the timeline for implementing the legislation

title: Congress seeks all-party meet on Women’s Reservation Act
content: Government reaches out again, this time in writing, after Congress spurns its informal feelers to discuss the implementation timeline for the Women’s Reservation Act

title: Congress Isolated as SP, DMK engage with government on women’s quota rollout
content: While Congress demands an all-party meeting on the Women’s Reservation Act, key INDIA bloc allies meet Amit Shah and signal support for government’s proposal

title: T.N. CM Stalin calls for constitutional safeguards to retain current share of States’ representation in delimitation
content: The current proportional representation of States should not be disturbed under any circumstances, says Chief Minister; Statement comes in the wake of reports that 2011 Census may be used for women’s reservation in Parliament and Legislative Assemblies

title: Congress accuses Modi govt. of doing U-turn on implementation of women’s reservation law
content: Move marks reversal of the government’s earlier stand that the provisions of the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam could only be operationalised after the completion of the next Census and delimitation exercise, it says

title: Madras High Court dismisses Tamannaah Bhatia’s plea for ₹1-crore damages from Power Soaps
content: She had accused the company of having exploited her popularity even after the expiry of the agreement period, but failed to prove it

title: T.N. CM Stalin accuses Centre of using delimitation to divert attention from cooking gas shortage
content: “Is the supply of gas cylinders and foreign policy under the control of the State government?” he questioned

title: Kerala Assembly Polls 2026 | No women, no vote: movement calls for NOTA revolt
content: Thulya Prathinidhya Prasthanam urges voters to back women candidates or opt for NOTA, slams fronts for ‘systematic exclusion’

title: Centre rejects Congress call to defer all-party meet on Women’s Reservation Act

title: Congress flags Registrar General’s remarks, says no reason for “bulldozing” Constitution amendment bills
content: Census Commissioner Mritunjay Kumar Narayan urges people to give accurate information to Census enumerators

title: Delimitation row: Revanth Reddy wants current gap in seats be maintained, as 50% increase in Lok Sabha seats will leave South at a disadvantage
content: If the 50% model is applied uniformly, the five southern States collectively would gain aroud 66 seats, while Hindi-belt and northern States, would see a rise of roughly 142 seats, Mr. Reddy said

title: TVK chief Vijay booked by Chennai police for poll code violations during campaign in city
content: The cases were filed based on complaints from election observers and flying squad officials, who stated that the TVK campaign events breached the conditions imposed under the Model Code of Conduct

title: Government proposing to up Lok Sabha size by 50%; move will disadvantage southern states: Congress
content: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh said Telangana Chief Minister Revanth Reddy has already raised an alarm over the issue and others may very well follow as this proposal becomes officially public

title: Delimitation: Vizag wards rise to 120, Vijayawada to have 86, Tirupati 66
content: Guntur, Nellore, Kurnool, Anantapur and other Urban Local Bodies in Andhra Pradesh also see significant rise in the number of wards based on population, as per MAUD Department’s circular for delimitation

title: Eight pilgrims from Chikkamagaluru killed, 12 hurt as vehicle rams truck near Mantralayam in Kurnool district
content: Nearly 20 persons from Chikkamagaluru district in neighbouring Karnataka were on their way to Sri Raghavendra Swamy Mutt, Mantralayam

title: Congress calls delimitation move ‘dangerous,’ says women’s law amended to grab political mileage in polls

title: Heatwave tightens grip in Telangana as nine districts record above 43℃; over 42℃ in 22 districts

title: Weapon of mass distraction: Congress slams Govt.’s delimitation proposal
content: Jairam Ramesh says the PM is ‘hoodwinking’ people by claiming that the strength of the southern States in the Lok Sabha will not go down. Siddaramaiah says five southern States together gain barely 63-66 additional seats, while just seven BJP-dominated States gain about 128-131 seats – nearly double

title: Fresh census a must for women’s quota, says Akhilesh

title: Modi pitches 33% reservation to win over women voters in West Bengal
